title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions. This proposal has sparked both excitement and curiosity within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI through an asset-financing agreement. This deal marks ReOrbit's expansion in the small GEO satellite market.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract to supply a satellite bus for a Japanese technology demonstration mission. This deal marks Apex's expansion in the Japanese space market.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract with the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic test flights over four years. This deal highlights the growing demand for hypersonic technology in national defense.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 25 commercial players
summary: Space Command will conduct a classified wargame involving 25 commercial players to improve collaboration and understand commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance joint operations between military and commercial space sectors.

title: An AI cyberattack could trigger a satellite apocalypse in the next 2 years. Are we prepared?
summary: AI-powered cyberattacks could hijack satellites and cause catastrophic collisions within two years, potentially making Earth's orbit unsafe. Experts warn that current defenses may not be prepared for such threats.

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit today
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite named 'Strix' today. The launch can be watched live.

title: You can stream the two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes when travelling, save 73% and claim a free $50 voucher when you sign up for two years of NordVPN
summary: Two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes will be available on BBC iPlayer in the UK. NordVPN is offering a 73% discount and a $50 voucher for a two-year subscription.

title: Watch NASA roll out Artemis 2 moon rocket tonight ahead of April 1 launch
summary: NASA is rolling out its Artemis 2 moon rocket to the launch pad on March 19 ahead of a planned April 1 liftoff. The event will be broadcast live for public viewing.

title: 'At the edge of what we thought possible': Astronomers find extremely rare star from ancient universe
summary: Astronomers discovered an extremely rare, iron-deficient second-generation star from the ancient universe, offering insights into how early stars enriched subsequent generations. This finding is hailed as a breakthrough in understanding cosmic archaeology.

title: NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has successfully completed final prelaunch tests, including extreme sound exposure and vibration tests, to ensure it can withstand launch conditions. The mission remains on track for an upcoming launch.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Abundant rainfall in February and March 2026 turned the desert landscape of Central Australia green. This transformation highlights the region's changing climate dynamics.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying contacts between the boxwork unit and layered sulfate unit on Mars. The mission is led by Catherine O'Connell-Cooper from the University of New Brunswick, Canada.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: NASA's Landsat 8 captured a rare clear image of Mayon, the most active volcano in the Philippines, on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows using infrared observations. The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS) reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds during the event.

title: From Service to Space Systems: A Pathways Journey to NASA
summary: Corey Elmore transitioned from service to a NASA Pathways engineering intern role at Kennedy Space Center, gaining hands-on experience in engineering environments through the program.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This brings the vehicle closer to its upcoming launch.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: This week features nine scheduled launches, including missions by SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum. The launches are part of a busy period in the space industry.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a new mission concept aimed at planetary defense. The hybrid system is designed to protect Earth from near-Earth objects.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for lunar and Martian missions by expanding production at its Starfactory facility. The company is transforming steel rolls into Starship components to support these ambitious goals.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) welcomed new crew members in February following the early departure of Crew-11 due to a medical concern. The month was marked by new arrivals and scientific activities aboard the ISS.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details of the news were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. This publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and science.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers in financial proposals for ESA Tender Actions.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation for an Aries satellite bus, which will be used for a communications demonstration mission next year. This deal marks Apex's entry into the Japanese space market.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket is undergoing modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, with the launch expected this summer. Changes were planned before a recent anomaly, and the company is accelerating these updates.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, secured $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce global reliance on satellite infrastructure. The investment highlights growing interest in alternative navigation technologies.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another step in its global expansion strategy. The Lithuanian company has found strong demand in the South Korean space industry.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it won't cause interference. The company claims the plan aligns with its existing L-band coordination.

title: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics to Build Solar Arrays for K-LEO
summary: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics will jointly supply solar arrays for K-LEO, South Korea’s sovereign Low-Earth Orbit national security constellation. The collaboration highlights advancements in space technology for national security applications.

title: Connectivity Under Fire: The Critical Need for Satcom Terminal Survivability
summary: The article discusses the critical importance of satellite communication (Satcom) terminal survivability in contested environments for U.S. military operations. It highlights the role of beyond-line-of-sight connectivity in maintaining command, control, and safety-of-life communications.

title: Satellogic Plans Next-Gen Merlin EO Constellation
summary: Satellogic unveiled plans for its next-generation 'Merlin' Earth observation satellites, which will offer one-meter resolution imaging. The constellation aims to provide daily global mapping capabilities.

title: 9 New Faces for SATShow Week 2026
summary: SATShow Week 2026 introduces nine new speakers, expanding its program to include more military representatives as GovMilSpace enters its second year.
